methodsAttempted.query: site:ai.upm.edu.my UPM AI HUB guideline generative AI teaching learning advisory note playbook
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl found the AI Hub guideline page, but both Firecrawl and direct HTTP fetch attempts timed out, so it was not used as claim evidence.
The SGS PDF was fetched from an official UPM subdomain; robots.txt returned only a crawl delay and no disallow rule.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found one accessible, policy-specific UPM source: the School of Graduate Studies PDF guideline for generative AI use in postgraduate thesis and viva contexts. The AI Hub page was inaccessible during this run, the CADe-Lead article was broader commentary rather than a binding policy source, and the UPM repository PDF was a research article rather than university policy evidence.

targets.sourceTitle: Guideline for Managing Generative Artificial Intelligence Usage in Postgraduate Thesis

stopConditions: Use only accessible official UPM sources with substantive AI policy or guidance text.
Reject official pages that are commentary, research-only, generic hubs, or inaccessible during the run.
Do not infer undergraduate, university-wide coursework, or general staff policy from postgraduate thesis-specific evidence.

artifacts.errorReason: Firecrawl scrape timed out; direct HTTPS and HTTP fetch attempts also timed out before content or robots.txt could be retrieved.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guideline for Managing Generative Artificial Intelligence Usage in Postgraduate Thesis

artifacts.claimText: UPM's postgraduate thesis generative AI guideline says postgraduate students must demonstrate that thesis intellectual contributions are predominantly their own despite AI assistance, and must ensure accuracy, references, originality, mastery, and control over the research process.

citation.publisher: Universiti Putra Malaysia School of Graduate Studies

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guideline for Managing Generative Artificial Intelligence Usage in Postgraduate Thesis
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students MUST: a) demonstrate that the intellectual contributions of the thesis are predominantly their own, despite AI assistance b) ensure the accuracy of facts and claims in the thesis, supported by appropriate evidence and references c) ensure the originality of the content in their thesis d) demonstrate a comprehensive understanding and mastery of their research contributions e) demonstrate that he/she can maintain control over his/her research process, and AI was used as a supporting tool

artifacts.title: Universiti Putra Malaysia AI Policy Staged Crawl Summary
artifacts.summary: Staged OpenClaw run for Universiti Putra Malaysia using official UPM sources only. The run found citation-ready evidence in the SGS UPM guideline for generative AI usage in postgraduate thesis and viva contexts, covering student responsibility for original contribution, attribution and AI-use disclosure, ethical data handling, viva declaration, and the thesis AI declaration form. The UPM AI Hub page was inaccessible during the run; a CADe-Lead article and a repository research paper were rejected as non-policy evidence.
limitations: Claims are scoped to UPM postgraduate thesis and viva guidance; this staged bundle does not establish university-wide undergraduate coursework, staff, procurement, or research-computing AI policy.
The UPM AI Hub guideline page was discovered but inaccessible during this run, so it was not used for claims.
This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
